mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods show no significant outperformance vs baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates above 9.5% signal deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and GDP growth signal weakening supply-side capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced corporate profitability.
failure_condition: Combined INDPRO/GDPC1 signals fail to produce negative SPX returns in 50%+ of cases over 3-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales forecasts indicates strengthening consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: HOUST/RSAFS growth fails to correlate with SPX 60d returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% erodes real interest rates, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant positive correlation with CPIAUCSL above 2.5% threshold (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing permits below 450k signal reduced construction activity, lowering demand for industrial goods and creating negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 450k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in payroll employment and rising unemployment rates indicate labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced consumer spending.
failure_condition: PAYEMS/UNRATE signals fail to produce negative SPX returns in 50%+ of cases over 3-year backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates above 4.5% signal monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% threshold.
